description: Quiz testing comprehension of 'The Real Crusoe' chapter about Alexander Selkirk and the origins of Robinson Crusoe for Class 6 students.

questions.question: Who was the real person whose story inspired Daniel Defoe to write Robinson Crusoe?
questions.explanation: Alexander Selkirk was a Scottish sailor whose true story of survival on Juan Fernandez Island directly inspired Daniel Defoe to create the fictional novel Robinson Crusoe.

questions.question: Why did Alexander Selkirk choose to land on Juan Fernandez Island?
options: He wanted to find treasure on the island
He had a disagreement with Captain William Dampier about the ship's safety and voyage conditions
He was searching for new land to colonize
He accidentally fell overboard and swam to the island
questions.correctAnswer: He had a disagreement with Captain William Dampier about the ship's safety and voyage conditions
questions.explanation: Selkirk had a heated argument with Captain William Dampier regarding the safety of the vessel and the conditions of the journey, which led him to decide to be put ashore on the island.

questions.question: Which captain rescued Alexander Selkirk from the island?
questions.explanation: Captain Woodes Rogers rescued Selkirk from Juan Fernandez Island during one of his voyages and later wrote about Selkirk's adventure in his book 'A Cruising Voyage Round the World.'

questions.question: In what year was the novel Robinson Crusoe published?
questions.explanation: Daniel Defoe published the fictional novel Robinson Crusoe in 1719, based on the real-life story of Alexander Selkirk.

questions.question: Alexander Selkirk spent approximately four years and four months alone on Juan Fernandez Island.
questions.explanation: According to the chapter content, Selkirk lived alone on the deserted Juan Fernandez Island for about four years and four months before being rescued.

questions.question: Robinson Crusoe is a work of fiction based on the real-life story of Alexander Selkirk.
questions.explanation: While Robinson Crusoe was inspired by Selkirk's true story, the novel itself is fictional, with the character Robinson Crusoe and the events in the story created by Daniel Defoe's imagination.
